summary:
The muscles of mastication—such as the masseter and temporalis—are primarily supplied with blood by branches of the maxillary artery, especially from its third part. However, since the available evidence lacks specific details on arterial segments related to these muscles, definitive confirmation based on retrieval data is absent.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on direct anatomical facts; it avoids speculative interpretations beyond established physiology.) 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Third part of the maxillary artery}}$
